description_long: This object is a vintage wall-mounted corded telephone, featuring a tall, rectangular wooden case with a glass front panel. The upper section houses a circular earpiece, while the lower section contains a mouthpiece, both made of dark metal or Bakelite. The left side of the case has a protruding, separate handset connected to the main body by a visible cord, and a rotary dial is visible behind the glass. The wood exterior is textured and appears aged, with visible grain and wear, giving it an antique appearance. The phone's design suggests it was intended for mounting on a vertical surface, likely in a public or semi-public setting.
description: A vintage wall-mounted corded telephone with a wooden case, glass front, rotary dial, and a separate handset attached by a cord.
description_view_0: Front view showing the glass panel, rotary dial, earpiece, mouthpiece, and the side-mounted handset.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thickness of the wooden case and a small protruding knob.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the textured wooden back and the side-mounted handset.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the handset and the cord attachment point.
